mechanism_narrative: A persistent federal funds rate at or above 5% compresses equity risk premia via elevated discount rates, producing a structural downward drag on S&P 500 forward returns that decays only as FEDFUNDS declines. The relationship is continuous and always active, not conditioned on a crossing event.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20d forward returns is not significantly negative (p>0.05) over rolling 2-year windows, or high-rate periods show returns statistically indistinguishable from low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes equity drawdowns as markets price recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive mean 20d forward returns or no statistically significant difference from unconditional baseline (t-test p>0.05) across at least two distinct historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) combined with a high policy rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly signal demand resilience under restrictive monetary conditions; when RSAFS growth remains positive despite FEDFUNDS above 5%, markets interpret this as evidence of a soft landing, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. Neither series alone captures this effect - RSAFS strength is only bullish when it persists despite tight policy.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with resilient consumer demand
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to outperform single-factor RSAFS-only signal by a statistically meaningful margin (rank IC improvement < 0.02), indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening (FEDFUNDS elevated), a month-over-month decline in INDPRO signals emerging industrial slack consistent with policy-induced slowdown, and markets react with short-horizon negative drift in the following week as growth concerns are re-priced.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines under tight policy are not significantly more negative than unconditional 5-day returns (p>0.05) across rolling samples.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects underlying economic expansion and corporate earnings resilience, supporting a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 prices over a quarterly horizon as labor income growth sustains consumption and profit expectations.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing PAYEMS growth and 60d forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or effect reverses sign in more than one historical expansion.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation accelerates above levels consistent with the Fed's implicit target while FEDFUNDS is already elevated, markets price increased probability of additional tightening or prolonged restrictive policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as valuation multiples compress further.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation persistence
failure_condition: Triggered episodes show 20d forward returns not significantly below unconditional baseline (t-test p>0.05), or effect reverses in more than one occurrence.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly signals interest-rate-sensitive demand destruction feeding into broader growth concerns, triggering an immediate short-horizon negative reaction in equities as the housing channel of monetary transmission becomes visible. The effect requires both a weak housing print and restrictive rates simultaneously.
failure_condition: Joint-trigger 5d forward returns are not significantly more negative than returns following HOUST decline alone (interaction term contributes rank IC improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime, real GDP (GDPC1) growth deceleration below trend signals that restrictive policy is successfully slowing aggregate demand, and equity markets price in a higher probability of margin compression, producing negative forward returns over the following quarter; in an easing or neutral regime, the same GDP deceleration is interpreted as counter-cyclical stabilization support and produces neutral-to-positive returns, making the direction of the effect conditional on the prevailing policy regime.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth decelerates below 1.5%
failure_condition: GDP deceleration under tightening regime fails to produce significantly negative 60d forward returns relative to deceleration episodes under easing regimes (difference-in-means p>0.05).
